====Pokémon==== This listing is of Raihan's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: =====On hand===== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Duraludon |giga=yes |type1=Steel |type2=Dragon |img=Raihan Duraludon.png |cap=Raihan's Duraludon |img2=Raihan Gigantamax Duraludon.png |cap2=Raihan's Gigantamax Duraludon |epnum=JN027 |epname=Toughing It Out! |vajp=Yūichi Nakamura |vaen=Billy Kametz |vaennote=[[JN027]]-[[JN045]] |desc={{p|Duraludon}} is Raihan's first known Pokémon, as well as his ace. It is capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing. Raihan used it in his battle against {{an|Leon}}, where it went up against {{TP|Leon|Charizard}}, only to be defeated. In ''[[JN045|Sword and Shield... The Legends Awaken!]]'', Raihan used Duraludon to defeat the Pokémon of [[Oleana's subordinate]] and other [[Macro Cosmos]] {{tc|Macro Cosmos's|employees}}. Later, after the Darkest Day ended, Duraludon was seen battling a Dynamaxed {{p|Kommo-o}}. In ''[[JN109|Aim For The Eight!]]'', Duraludon was the final Pokémon used in Raihan's World Coronation Series match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against {{AP|Lucario}}. Despite its powerful [[Max Move]]s, Duraludon was defeated by Lucario's giant {{m|Aura Sphere}}. Duraludon's known moves are {{m|Metal Claw}}, {{m|Iron Defense}}, {{m|Stone Edge}}, {{m|Steel Beam}}, and {{m|Breaking Swipe}}.
As a Gigantamax Duraludon, it can use {{m|Max Rockfall}}, {{m|Max Steelspike}}, and {{m|G-Max Depletion}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Flygon |type1=Ground |type2=Dragon |img=Raihan Flygon.png |epnum=JN109 |epname=Aim For The Eight! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Flygon}} is Raihan's second known Pokémon. It was the first Pokémon that Raihan used in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against his {{AP|Dragonite}} and won, before losing to [[Gigantamax]] {{AP|Gengar}}. Flygon's known moves are {{m|Dragon Claw}}, {{m|Steel Wing}}, {{m|Sandstorm}}, and {{m|Crunch}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Goodra |type1=Dragon |img=Raihan Goodra.png |epnum=JN109 |epname=Aim For The Eight! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Goodra}} is Raihan's third known Pokémon. It was the second Pokémon that Raihan used in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against his [[Gigantamax]] {{AP|Gengar}}. The confrontation between the two Pokémon eventually led to a double knockout. Goodra's known moves are {{m|Rain Dance}}, {{m|Thunder}}, {{m|Surf}}, and {{m|Hydro Pump}}.}} =====Temporary===== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Alcremie |gender=female |type1=Fairy |img=Raihan Alcremie.png |epnum=JN082 |epname=The Sweet Taste of Battle! |vajp= |vaen=Ryan Bartley |desc=Raihan borrowed an {{p|Alcremie}} from [[Opal]] to take part in the [[All-Alcremie Decoration Challenge]]. None of Alcremie's moves are known.}} =====Traveling with===== {| width="100%" |- | {{ActivePoké|||nick=Rotom Phone|Raihan Rotom Phone.png|electric|ghost|link=Rotom Phone}} | | |}
